full_prompt: Design the accumulator container (battery enclosure) for a 2026 collegiate hybrid formula-class racecar benchmark + Electric competition vehicle. The container inherits collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark / collegiate formula-class racecar chassis and IA rules but must additionally satisfy collegiate hybrid formula-class racecar benchmark accumulator rules: (1) tractive system voltage <= 300 V DC; (2) isolation resistance between tractive system and grounded container >= 500 ohm/V; (3) the container shall survive the same 40 g forward / 20 g side / 20 g vertical inertial envelope as collegiate formula-class racecar. Accumulator cells: Li-ion 18650, 288 cells in 12S24P configuration, pack mass 15 kg, nominal voltage 43.2 V (module) or 216 V (full tractive system). The container is a welded aluminum 6061-T6 box, wall thickness 3 mm typical, E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. Outer bounding box 400 mm (X) x 300 mm (Y) x 150 mm (Z). Mounted to chassis via 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts on 350 mm x 250 mm rectangular pattern. Load cases: (R1) Forward 40 g on 15 kg pack = 40 x 15 x 9.81 = 5,886 N; max von Mises <= yield / 1.1 = 251 MPa. (R2) Side 20 g = 2,943 N; max von Mises <= 251 MPa. (R3) Vertical 20 g = 2,943 N (both +Z and -Z); max von Mises <= 251 MPa. (R4) Isolation-resistance constraint: no bolt hole or structural path shall create a metal-to-metal contact between the cells and the chassis-bonded container (insulating liner >= 1 mm PET, ensured by geometric keep-out >= 2 mm from each cell envelope); this is a geometric constraint, not a stress test. (R5) Container mass <= 5 kg. (R6) First modal frequency >= 40 Hz. (R7) If a cell vents, the container shall have >= 300 mm^2 total vent area on the top to release pressure (safety requirement). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3) + modal. Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded container, weld map, insulation/vent callouts, and a report listing container mass in kg, max von Mises per load case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz, vent area in mm^2.
short_prompt:
Design the accumulator container (battery enclosure) for a 2026 collegiate hybrid formula-class racecar, inheriting collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark chassis / IA rules plus hybrid-class accumulator rules: tractive-system voltage <= 300 V DC, isolation resistance between tractive system and grounded container >= 500 ohm/V, 40 g forward / 20 g side / 20 g vertical inertial envelope. Container is a welded 6061-T6 aluminum box (~3 mm walls), mounted via 4 x M8 Grade 8.8 on a 350 x 250 mm rectangular pattern. Accumulator: Li-ion 18650, 288 cells in 12S24P, pack mass 15 kg, module 43.2 V / system 216 V nominal.

Envelope: outer bounding 400 x 300 x 150 mm (X x Y x Z). Isolation liner >= 1 mm PET with >= 2 mm keep-out from each cell envelope.

Load cases (inertial loads on the 15 kg pack).
- LC1 forward 40 g: 5,886 N.
- LC2 side 20 g: 2,943 N.
- LC3 vertical 20 g (+Z and -Z): 2,943 N.
- LC4 modal: first frequency >= 40 Hz.
- LC5 safety venting: if a cell vents, total top-face vent area >= 300 mm^2 (geometric).

prompt.title: collegiate hybrid formula-class racecar benchmark accumulator container
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 welded accumulator box, 15 kg Li-ion pack, inherits collegiate formula-class racecar 40/20/20 g envelope plus isolation + vent area constraints.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four M8 bolt bosses fully fixed

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal runaway out of scope at this gate.

eval_coverage: inherited collegiate formula-class racecar 40/20/20 g envelope
